capital_req_001.raw_text: Banks must maintain a minimum capital of 1,500,000 INR.

kyc_process_001.raw_text: Customer due diligence shall be completed within 30 days of account opening.

aml_threshold_001.raw_text: Suspicious transactions exceeding 1,00,000 INR must be reported within 24 hours.

cybersecurity_001.raw_text: All financial institutions must implement multi-factor authentication (MFA) for customer-facing portals.

treasury_liquidity_001.raw_text: Banks must maintain a minimum Liquidity Coverage Ratio (LCR) of 100% of net cash outflows for a 30-day stress period.

data_privacy_001.raw_text: Customer personal data must be encrypted at rest using AES-256 encryption or equivalent.

stress_testing_001.raw_text: Banks must conduct quarterly stress tests on their credit portfolio under adverse market conditions.

board_oversight_001.raw_text: The Board must review risk management policies and procedures at least once per quarter.

cyber_incident_001.raw_text: All cyber incidents must be reported to the regulator within 48 hours of discovery.

export_credit_001.raw_text: Export credit exposure should not exceed 5% of total advances for any single country.

compliance_maps.summary: New high impact regulatory clause added
compliance_maps.new_obligation: In partial modification of the A.P. (DIR Series) Circular No. 13 dated June 8, 2026 on ‘NOP- INR position of Authorised Dealer Category-I banks’, it has been decided that AD Cat-I banks shall exclude the positions arising out of hedged transactions related to FCNR (B) deposits, External Commercial Borrowings and Overseas Foreign Currency Borrowings raised in terms of the aforesaid circulars, while ensuring compliance with the provisions of the A.P. (DIR Series) Circular No. 24 dated March 27, 2026 and for computation of net overnight open position in terms of the aforesaid Master Direction.

compliance_maps.summary: New medium impact regulatory clause added
compliance_maps.new_obligation: The directions contained in this circular have been issued under Sections 10(4), 11(1) and 11(2) of the FEMA, 1999 (42 of 1999) and are without prejudice to permissions / approvals, if any, required under any other law. Yours faithfully, (Dimple Bhandia) Chief General Manager
